Active Project

AI Personal Assistant

A voice-activated personal assistant built on the Claude API, designed to handle everyday tasks through natural conversation while remembering context across sessions.

Role
Solo developer
Stack
Claude API, FastAPI, React
Status
Actively maintained

The problem

Most "AI assistant" demos are stateless — every conversation starts from zero, and the assistant forgets what you told it five minutes ago. I wanted something closer to a real assistant: one that could hold context across a session and actually take action, not just respond conversationally.

What I built

A voice-activated assistant powered by the Claude API, with a FastAPI backend handling intent routing and a React.js frontend for the conversational interface. The system recognizes and routes across 5+ intent categories, including task management, calendar scheduling, and web search.

The core engineering challenge was persistent contextual memory — keeping the assistant grounded in what had already been discussed, rather than treating every message in isolation.

How it works

Voice input is transcribed and passed to FastAPI, which classifies intent before routing to the right handler. Each handler calls the Claude API with relevant session context attached, and the React frontend keeps replies and confirmations flowing naturally.

Active Project

IAM Policy Analyzer CLI

A Python command-line tool that audits IAM users, roles, and policies across multiple AWS accounts — built to automate work I was doing manually at AWS.

Role
Solo developer
Stack
Python, AWS IAM, boto3
Accounts covered
10+

The problem

At AWS, I led a manual IAM audit across 70+ users and 50+ accounts — slow, repetitive, and error-prone. Unused credentials quietly pile up as a security risk when reviews aren't automated.

What I built

A Python CLI that connects to AWS IAM across multiple accounts and audits users, roles, and policies in one pass, automating detection of:

  • Unused permissions — policies attached but not exercised recently
  • Stale credentials — keys and users with no active usage
  • Cross-account inconsistencies — permission drift between accounts

How it works

The CLI authenticates against each target account via the AWS SDK, pulls IAM policy documents and usage data, and cross-references granted permissions against actual service usage from access logs — turning a multi-day manual audit into a single command.

City of Orlando Internship

Downtown Orlando Parking Guide

A city-wide web tool built during my internship with the City of Orlando, helping residents and visitors find parking across downtown more easily.

Role
Software Engineer Intern
Stack
HTML, CSS, PHP, Node.js
Timeline
Aug 2023 – Apr 2024

The problem

Downtown Orlando's parking information was scattered and hard to navigate. The City wanted a public-facing tool that consolidated availability, rates, and locations into something fast and genuinely usable.

What I built

A web-based parking guide using HTML, CSS, PHP, and Node.js, serving city-wide users. The core focus was data retrieval performance — I optimized the JSON parsing layer powering the guide, meaningfully improving load times as the dataset grew.

Working with the city

This was built in close collaboration with city stakeholders, making sure the tool reflected how residents actually wanted to find and use parking information.

City of Orlando Internship

Accessibility Chrome Extension

A Chrome extension born out of UX research, designed to flag and fix accessibility gaps in city websites.

Role
Software Engineer Intern
Stack
JavaScript, Chrome Extension API
Result
+35% accessibility

The problem

Before writing any code, I conducted UX research on the City of Orlando's web properties — journey maps and usability analysis. A recurring pattern emerged: missing or poorly written alt-tags, and wording that wasn't accessible to screen reader users.

What I built

A Chrome extension focused on alt-tag and wording improvements for city websites. It scans page content, flags accessibility gaps, and surfaces specific suggestions — measured at a 35% increase in website accessibility against the city's existing pages.

Working with stakeholders

I collaborated with 5+ cross-functional stakeholders across Orlando Police, Sustainability, and Fire departments, delivering iterative releases following Agile methodologies as the tool evolved.
